evals.prompt: Set up Prettier, ESLint, EditorConfig, and format-on-save for this project (sample-node-project — a plain Node project, no framework, no TypeScript, no existing config).
evals.expected_output: Creates a flat ESLint config appropriate to a plain Node project (no Next.js/TypeScript-specific config), adds lint/format npm scripts, .prettierrc, .editorconfig, and .vscode/settings.json + extensions.json.
expectations: Creates eslint.config.mjs using @eslint/js (not the Next.js plugin, since next isn't a dependency, and not typescript-eslint, since there's no tsconfig.json)
Adds a `lint` script (eslint .) and a `format` script (prettier --write .) to package.json
Creates .vscode/settings.json with editor.formatOnSave true and the ESLint fixAll code action, plus .vscode/extensions.json recommending the ESLint and Prettier extensions
Creates .editorconfig with the documented indent/charset/line-ending settings
Does not attempt any TypeScript-specific ESLint setup since no tsconfig.json is present

evals.prompt: Set up Prettier and ESLint for this project (sample-node-project-with-prettierrc — same as before, but it already has a .prettierrc).
evals.expected_output: Leaves the existing .prettierrc untouched and reports its current contents instead of overwriting it.
expectations: Does not overwrite the existing .prettierrc (printWidth 120, semi true)
Reports the existing .prettierrc contents back to the user instead of silently replacing them
Still proceeds with the other setup steps (ESLint config, scripts, editorconfig, vscode settings)
